channel.title: Lionpoint Partners — Market Pulse
channel.description: Daily read on the US legal lateral market.

item.title: Debevoise pulls a Ropes & Gray finance partner, and Robins Kaplan grabs four ex-DOJ lawyers
item.description: Debevoise is bringing in a Ropes and Gray finance partner in New York to build out its finance bench. Robins Kaplan added four former DOJ attorneys in...

item.title: Paul Weiss lands the partner who built Kirkland's restructuring practice
item.description: Paul Weiss is bringing in the partner credited with building Kirkland's restructuring practice, one of the most recognized bankruptcy names in the...

item.title: Davis Polk pulls Hogan Lovells' M&A chief days before the Cadwalader merger closes
item.description: Davis Polk hired the global head of Hogan Lovells' M&A practice as a partner in Washington, and he is walking out after three decades just days before...

item.title: White & Case keeps buying, and Goodwin loses two partners in one day
item.description: White & Case hired the co-leader of Goodwin's secondaries practice as a partner in New York, part of an aggressive US buildout that has brought on more...

item.title: Paul Hastings hits eight PE partners in eight months, and the deal-side wire stays hot
item.description: Paul Hastings pulled a private equity partner out of Dechert in New York, and by the firm's own count that's the eighth PE partner it has added in eight...

item.title: A finance co-head is reportedly headed to Dechert, and state AGs circle the Paramount deal
item.description: The bigger headline is in the courts, where California's attorneys general are reportedly looking at two former FTC lawyers at Milbank to help challenge...

item.title: Houston turns into a deal-lawyer dogfight, and firms race to staff up on dealmakers
item.description: Simpson Thacher pulled an energy and infrastructure deals partner out of Latham in Houston, and Latham also added a capital markets partner from A&O...

item.title: Sidley pulls a finance partner out of Wachtell, and DC antitrust hiring runs hot
item.description: The poach of the day was Sidley pulling a global finance partner out of Wachtell in New York. Washington was all about antitrust: Sullivan and Cromwell...

item.title: Skadden lifts an investment management group out of Akin, and funds hiring runs hot
item.description: Busy day on the lateral wire. Skadden poached a three-partner investment management group out of Akin Gump, and New York kept rolling: Simpson Thacher...

item.title: California employment hiring heats up, and Paul Weiss draws a malpractice suit
item.description: Labor and employment hiring picked up on the West Coast. Jackson Lewis brought on three wage and hour litigators from Gordon Rees in Los Angeles, and...

item.title: McGuireWoods builds in Houston energy, and Ohio moves to rein in litigation finance
item.description: Quieter Friday on the wire, but the Texas energy build keeps going. McGuireWoods added an energy M&A and finance partner in Houston, hired out of...

item.title: Mayer Brown raids McGuireWoods again for Texas, and salaries keep climbing
item.description: Quieter day on the wire. Mayer Brown pulled another McGuireWoods partner, an energy and M&A lawyer, in Houston as it pushes hard to grow in Texas. Steptoe...

item.title: The road to equity partner keeps narrowing, and Foley raids Sidley
item.description: The road to equity partner keeps getting narrower. Bloomberg Law says the biggest firms barely grew their equity ranks last year while non-equity partners...

item.title: Gibson Dunn buys its way to number two in M&A, and Taft lifts a team out of Stinson
item.description: Plenty else moved too. Taft lifted a three-partner group out of Stinson in Denver, DLA Piper added a venture capital partner in Palo Alto, K&L Gates...

item.title: The price of a rainmaker keeps climbing, and Gibson Dunn raids Kirkland
item.description: The price of a rainmaker keeps going up. Bloomberg reports top partners are now commanding around thirty million dollars, with more of that pay guaranteed...

item.title: The IPO wave hits Big Law, and Willkie raids Goodwin
item.description: Another busy day in the lateral market. Willkie pulled a two-partner private equity team out of Goodwin in Los Angeles and San Francisco, Skadden added an...

item.title: Cooley raids Kirkland, and partner pay tops $40M
item.description: The bigger story is comp. Top partner pay has now passed forty million dollars, and firms are growing their bonus pools while sharing less about how pay...

item.title: Weil at a crossroads, and a Paul Hastings poach
item.description: Plenty else moved too. Paul Hastings poached a capital markets team out of Winston Taylor in Dallas and Orange County, Norton Rose added a trade and...

item.title: Paul Weiss pulls energy out of Akin Gump in Houston
item.description: There was plenty else moving. Jones Day made two New York hires, a health care and life sciences partner and a top federal prosecutor from the Southern...

item.title: Epstein Becker Green pulls six out of Manatt
item.description: There was plenty else moving, too. Weil pulled an M&A partner back from Simpson Thacher, Cravath added a national security counsel in Washington after a...

item.title: Kirkland's $500M AI bet, and two named poaches
item.description: The biggest BigLaw story today: Kirkland is putting $500 million into building its own AI platform over the next three to four years. 180 tech people are...

item.title: A seven-hire New York day in BigLaw
item.description: Today the story in BigLaw is New York. Seven partner hires landed in the city in a single day, with the headline move being Wilson Sonsini pulling an M&A...

item.title: A hiring list too long for one section
item.description: Today the moves were the story. Bloomberg's partner-hiring list ran long enough to need a second section, so here's the quick run. Lowenstein added an M&A...

item.title: Private equity is moving into law firms
item.description: Bloomberg Law reports that private equity is moving aggressively into personal injury firms, offering lawyers a cut of the wealth those deals generate. At...

item.title: How one firm bought its way up the M&A rankings
item.description: Quiet Memorial Day in BigLaw, so just a quick read. It wasn't one blockbuster year. It was years of lateral hiring, pulling M&A partners from firms like...
